neighbor:
Include the following in your description: the role of RNA polymerase, the role of the promoter, and the role of the terminator.
Describe how DNA is transcribed into RNA in a cell. Include the following in your description: the role of RNA polymerase, the role of the promoter, and the role of the terminator.
Transcription is the process by which the information in a gene, encoded as a sequence of DNA nucleotides, is copied into a complementary sequence of RNA nucleotides. The process of transcription is divided into three phases: initiation, elongation, and termination. The initiation phase begins when RNA polymerase binds to a specific DNA sequence called the promoter. The promoter is located upstream of the gene and is recognized by RNA
